name: deployment-validation-config-validate
description: You are a configuration management expert specializing in validating, testing, and ensuring the correctness of application configurations. Create comprehensive validation schemas, implement configurat
capabilities: Analyze and validate configuration files in JSON, YAML, TOML, INI, and ENV formats
Generate JSON Schema definitions for configuration structure validation
Enforce environment-specific rules such as debug mode restrictions and HTTPS requirements
Detect potential secrets and hardcoded credentials in configuration files
Monitor configuration files at runtime for changes with automatic re-validation
Create version migration scripts for configuration schema evolution
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 37 patterns but all are false positives. The skill uses standard libraries (os, yaml, json, crypto, chokidar, ajv) for legitimate configuration validation. No command injection, no external network calls, no dynamic code execution. Cryptographic implementations use strong algorithms (aes-256-gcm, pbkdf2-sha256). File operations are scoped to configuration files only. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Configuration errors cause deployment failures and security vulnerabilities. This skill provides automated validation, schema enforcement, and environment-specific checks to prevent misconfigurations before they reach production.
userTitle: Validate deployment configurations with automated schemas
limitations: Validates file-based configurations only, not runtime application state
Requires configuration schemas to be defined for comprehensive validation
Cannot execute or test configurations in actual deployment environments
Secret detection uses pattern matching and may produce false positives

useCases.title: Pre-deployment Configuration Check
useCases.description: Validate all configuration files before deploying to production to catch errors early.

useCases.targetUser: Platform teams managing multiple deployment environments
useCases.title: Multi-environment Consistency
useCases.description: Ensure configuration remains consistent across development, staging, and production environments with environment-specific rules.

useCases.title: Configuration Security Audit
useCases.description: Scan configuration files for hardcoded secrets, weak encryption settings, and security misconfigurations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Configuration Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate my configuration files for syntax errors and schema compliance. Check the config directory and report any issues found.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick validation of existing configuration files

promptTemplates.title: Schema Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a JSON Schema for my configuration files that enforces required fields, data types, and format constraints for all environments.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating validation schemas for new projects

promptTemplates.title: Security Configuration Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my configuration files for security issues including hardcoded secrets, debug settings in production configs, and insecure defaults.

promptTemplates.title: Runtime Configuration Monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up runtime configuration validation that watches for file changes and automatically re-validates when configurations are updated.

bestPractices: Store configuration schemas in version control alongside configuration files
Define separate validation rules for each environment with stricter requirements for production
Run configuration validation as a mandatory step in CI/CD pipelines before deployment
antiPatterns: Committing actual secrets or credentials to configuration files in version control
Skipping configuration validation in automated deployment pipelines
Maintaining completely separate configuration files per environment without shared schema

faq.question: What configuration file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON, YAML, TOML, INI, and ENV file formats. It can detect and validate multiple formats within the same project.

faq.question: Can this generate validation schemas automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can analyze existing configuration files and generate JSON Schema definitions that capture the structure, required fields, and data type constraints.

faq.question: Does this support runtime configuration changes?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes file watching capabilities that detect configuration changes and automatically re-validate when files are modified.

faq.question: How does secret detection work?
faq.answer: The skill uses pattern matching to identify potential secrets like API keys, passwords, and tokens. It flags suspicious values for manual review to avoid false positives.

faq.question: Can I validate configurations for multiple environments?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports environment-specific validation rules. For example, it can enforce HTTPS and longer passwords in production while allowing relaxed settings in development.

faq.question: How do I handle configuration version migrations?
faq.answer: The skill can generate migration scripts that transform configurations from older schema versions to newer ones, maintaining backward compatibility during upgrades.
